places.name: Munnar Tea Gardens
places.description: Munnar is a hill station at 1,600 m altitude, blanketed in emerald-green tea plantations stretching across rolling hills. Home to some of the highest tea-growing estates in the world, the town offers misty mountain vistas, rare Neelakurinji flowers (blooming once every 12 years), and rich biodiversity.
places.history: Munnar was developed as a hill station by the British in the late 19th century. The tea plantations were established by the Finlay Muir Company in the 1870s. Today, the estates are managed by the Tata Group (Kannan Devan Hills Plantations).
places.famousFor: Tea plantations, Eravikulam National Park, Nilgiri Tahr, Neelakurinji flowers (next bloom: 2030), Mattupetty Dam
places.bestTime: September to May (December–February for mist; August–October for Neelakurinji if blooming year)
places.entryFee: Tea Museum: ₹125; Eravikulam NP: ₹125 Indians, ₹420 foreigners

places.name: Alleppey Backwaters
places.description: Alappuzha (Alleppey) is the "Venice of the East," famous for its labyrinthine network of backwater canals, lagoons, and lakes. Houseboat cruises through the palm-fringed waterways offer an enchanting glimpse into Kerala's traditional village life, paddy fields, and coconut groves.
places.history: Alleppey was developed as a major trading port by Diwan Surya Narayana Rao in the late 18th century. The famous Nehru Trophy Boat Race, held annually on Punnamada Lake since 1952, features traditional snake boats (chundan vallam) with 100+ oarsmen.
places.famousFor: Houseboat cruises, Nehru Trophy Boat Race, Punnamada Lake, Coir making, Rice paddies
places.bestTime: September to March (August for Snake Boat Race)
